response: TEMPERATURE IS A MEASURE OF THE HEAT ENERGY IN A SUBSTANCE. IT IS A PHYSICAL PROPERTY THAT IS INDEPENDENT OF THE AMOUNT OF THE SUBSTANCE. THE TEMPERATURE OF A BODY IS INFLUENCED BY THE RATE OF ENERGY TRANSFER DURING HEAT EXCHANGE. WHEN TWO SUBSTANCES ARE IN CONTACT, HEAT IS TRANSFERRED FROM THE HOTTER TO THE COLDER ONE. THIS PROCESS CONTINUES UNTIL BOTH SUBSTANCES REACH THE SAME TEMPERATURE. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SOLUTION CAN BE CALCULATED USING THE FORMULA: T = (m * C + m * C) / (M * V).
